title: lucilia ferruginea
intro: Lucilia ferruginea, also known as the Red Ironweed or Ironweed, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its iron-rich flowers, which have been used to treat anemia and other iron-deficiency disorders. The health benefits of Lucilia ferruginea include its ability to act as an expectorant, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of menopause and PMS.
